debug_python.name: debug_python
debug_python.description: Debug a Python script — find the error, explain why it happens, suggest a fix
required_context: What the code is supposed to do
approach_steps: Read the code carefully, understand its intent
Identify the error type (syntax, runtime, logic, import)
Trace the execution path to the failure point
Explain WHY the error occurs, not just what it is
Provide a corrected version of the code
Suggest how to prevent similar errors
debug_python.output_format: Problem identification → Root cause → Fixed code → Prevention tip

explain_concept.name: explain_concept
explain_concept.description: Explain a technical concept clearly, with examples and analogies
approach_steps: Start with a one-sentence definition
Give a concrete analogy from everyday life
Show a minimal working example
Explain the 'why' — when would you use this?
Point out common misconceptions
Suggest what to learn next
explain_concept.output_format: Definition → Analogy → Example → Use case → Gotchas → Next steps

write_function.name: write_function
write_function.description: Write a Python function to specification
approach_steps: Clarify the specification — ask if anything is ambiguous
Write the function with type hints and docstring
Handle edge cases explicitly
Write 3-5 test cases that verify correctness
Run the tests to prove it works
Suggest optimizations if relevant
write_function.output_format: Function with docstring → Test cases → Verified output

code_review.name: code_review
code_review.description: Review code for bugs, style issues, performance problems, and security concerns
approach_steps: Read for correctness — does it do what it claims?
Check for common bugs (off-by-one, null refs, race conditions)
Evaluate naming and readability
Look for performance issues (unnecessary loops, memory leaks)
Check for security concerns (injection, unsafe input handling)
Provide specific, actionable feedback with line references
code_review.output_format: Summary → Critical issues → Suggestions → Positive observations

refactor_code.name: refactor_code
refactor_code.description: Refactor existing code to be cleaner, more maintainable, or more performant
approach_steps: Understand current behavior completely
Identify specific code smells or issues
Plan refactoring steps (small, testable changes)
Apply changes incrementally
Verify behavior is preserved
Explain what changed and why

design_system.name: design_system
design_system.description: Design a software system or architecture from requirements
approach_steps: Clarify requirements and constraints
Identify core entities and their relationships
Define system boundaries and interfaces
Choose appropriate patterns and technologies
Draw out the key components and data flow
Identify risks and tradeoffs
Suggest an implementation roadmap
design_system.output_format: Requirements summary → Component diagram → Data flow → Tradeoffs → Roadmap

analyze_data.name: analyze_data
analyze_data.description: Analyze a dataset — find patterns, anomalies, and insights
approach_steps: Load and inspect the data (shape, types, nulls)
Compute basic statistics (mean, median, distribution)
Look for patterns and correlations
Identify outliers and anomalies
Generate visualizations if helpful
Summarize findings in plain language
Suggest follow-up questions
analyze_data.output_format: Data overview → Key statistics → Patterns → Anomalies → Insights → Next questions

write_tests.name: write_tests
write_tests.description: Write comprehensive tests for existing code
approach_steps: Identify all public functions and their contracts
Write happy-path tests for normal behavior
Write edge-case tests (empty input, max values, type errors)
Write failure-mode tests (what should raise exceptions?)
Run all tests and verify they pass
Report coverage gaps
write_tests.output_format: Test file → Test results → Coverage assessment

troubleshoot_environment.name: troubleshoot_environment
troubleshoot_environment.description: Help debug environment issues — packages, paths, configs, permissions
approach_steps: Gather environment info (Python version, OS, installed packages)
Reproduce the error if possible
Check common causes (wrong Python, missing package, path issues)
Provide step-by-step fix instructions
Verify the fix works
Explain root cause so it doesn't recur
troubleshoot_environment.output_format: Diagnosis → Root cause → Fix steps → Verification → Prevention

brainstorm.name: brainstorm
brainstorm.description: Generate ideas and explore possibilities for a project or problem
approach_steps: Understand the problem space fully
Generate 10+ ideas without filtering
Group ideas by theme or approach
Evaluate feasibility and impact of top ideas
Deep-dive on the 3 most promising
Suggest concrete next steps for each
brainstorm.output_format: Problem restatement → Idea list → Top 3 deep-dives → Recommended next step
